name: Sierra de Candelario Climbs Loop
name_original: Subida al Alto del Travieso - circular por Montemayor del Río — desde Baños de Montemayor
description: This difficult 67.7-mile road cycling route climbs to Alto del Travieso, offering panoramic views and challenging gradients through the Sier
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Baños De Montemayor traverse the diverse landscapes of the Ambroz Valley, characterized by its green valleys and mountain scenery. The region features extensive oak and chestnut forests, with routes often following natural paths like the Vía Verde de la Plata. Cyclists can encounter varied terrain, from gentle greenways to challenging climbs towards the Sierra de Béjar and Candelario mountain ranges. Best road cycling routes around Baños De Montemayor The most popular road cycling route is Ambroz and Jerte Valleys Loop via Puerto de Honduras, a 56.4 miles (90.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 17 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive climbs and panoramic views across two valleys. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de Béjar y Béjar desde Harvás – circular por Montemayor del Río, a difficult 49.3 miles (79.3 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain and passes through mountainous terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Sierra de Candelario Climbs Loop, a 67.7 miles (108.9 km) trail leading through the challenging Sierra de Candelario mountain range, often completed in about 6 hours 18 minutes. Road cycling around Baños De Montemayor is defined by the Ambroz Valley, extensive oak and chestnut forests, and the Vía Verde de la Plata. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Baños De Montemayor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 20 reviews. More than 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Baños De Montemayor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Baños de Montemayor?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes available around Baños de Montemayor, catering to various skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Baños de Montemayor?
A: The terrain around Baños de Montemayor is diverse, ranging from the generally easy and scenic paths of the Vía Verde de la Plata to challenging climbs in the Sierra de Béjar and Candelario mountain ranges. You'll cycle through green valleys, extensive oak and chestnut forests, and encounter significant elevation changes on more difficult routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Baños de Montemayor?
A: Baños de Montemayor enjoys a pleasant climate for much of the year, with mild winters and warm summers, making it suitable for cycling across many seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for exploring the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Baños de Montemayor?
A: Yes, Baños de Montemayor offers several routes suitable for beginners. There are 4 easy road cycling tours available, providing gentler terrain for those new to the sport or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: For challenging climbs, consider routes that venture into the mountainous terrain surrounding Baños de Montemayor. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023300023/ambroz-and-jerte-valleys-loop-via-puerto-de-honduras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ambroz and Jerte Valleys Loop via Puerto de Honduras&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023280187/sierra-de-candelario-climbs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Candelario Climbs Loop&lt;/a&gt; are excellent options, featuring extensive elevation gains and panoramic views. Another demanding route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056223805/puerto-de-bejar-y-bejar-desde-harvas-circular-por-montemayor-del-rio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Béjar y Béjar desde Harvás – circular por Montemayor del Río&lt;/a&gt;, which includes significant elevation gain through mountainous areas.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling in Baños de Montemayor?
A: While cycling, you can enjoy the stunning landscapes of the Ambroz Valley, with its green valleys and extensive oak and chestnut forests. Routes often offer views of the Baños de Montemayor Reservoir. For specific landmarks, you might pass by the historic Roman road or catch glimpses of the Sierra de Béjar and Candelario mountain ranges. Notable points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4762185" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Béjar Pass&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2812934" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;"La Cruz" on the ancient Roman road Calzada Baños de Montemayor&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes offer longer distances for endurance cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023280187/sierra-de-candelario-climbs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Candelario Climbs Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, covers nearly 109 km (67.7 miles), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023300023/ambroz-and-jerte-valleys-loop-via-puerto-de-honduras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ambroz and Jerte Valleys Loop via Puerto de Honduras&lt;/a&gt; is approximately 90 km (56.4 miles).

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Baños de Montemayor?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in the region are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056223805/puerto-de-bejar-y-bejar-desde-harvas-circular-por-montemayor-del-rio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Béjar y Béjar desde Harvás – circular por Montemayor del Río&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Béjar – View of Hervás loop from Hervás&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Baños de Montemayor?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from 20 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the green valleys and extensive forests to the challenging mountain climbs, and the well-maintained paths like the Vía Verde de la Plata.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or panoramic spots accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, many routes offer fantastic panoramic vistas. The climb towards Puerto de Béjar, for example, rewards riders with stunning views of the Ambroz Valley. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4762185" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Béjar Pass&lt;/a&gt; is a notable viewpoint, and routes through the Sierra de Candelario also provide expansive mountain scenery.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the cycling routes, or is parking generally accessible?
A: While specific public transport details for route access might vary, Baños de Montemayor is well-regarded for its outdoor activities, and accommodations often provide secure storage for bicycles. Parking is generally accessible within the town, serving as a convenient starting point for many routes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Baños de Montemayor?
A: For families or those seeking easier rides, the Vía Verde de la Plata offers scenic and generally easy cycling conditions, making it a great option. There are 4 easy road cycling tours available that are suitable for a more relaxed pace.
tips: Puerta de Extremadura entering from Salamanca. It is reached by a road with curves going downhill and you can enjoy the view of the town from above. Just before entering there is a characteristic horseshoe curve of the great cycling routes. Baños gets its name from the modern spa that today sits on the magnificently preserved remains of Roman baths.
After passing the town of Garganta and going up the slopes of the Sierra de Béjar, there are panoramic views of the Navamuño Reservoir.

Demanding 1st category port, with an average slope of 7.38%.
Worth the climb for the views alone

Altimetry: El Alto del Travieso is a port with very constant slopes and hardly any rest. In fact, we have 11 km with average inclines greater than 7%, although we will not find double-digit inclines on average. This is a tough climb, but without a maximum level of demand, which I consider highly recommendable.
Section of road that is located at 1080 meters and where it is possible to make a stop and refill the water bottle. If necessary, rest a bit...
Nice climb from Montemayor del Rio

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this route and what gear is recommended?
faqs.answer: This is classified as a difficult road cycling route, requiring a very good fitness level. The climb to Alto del Travieso is a first-category ascent, long and tough. It's highly recommended to use mountain gearing with a compact system, such as a 34-tooth front chainring and a 30, 32, or 34-tooth rear cassette, to manage the demanding gradients effectively.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is mostly paved and generally easy to ride, but be aware that some segments might be unpaved and more challenging. You'll cycle through diverse landscapes including areas with trees and mountains, exuberant chestnut forests, oak forests, and meadows, offering varied scenery throughout.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the charming town of Baños de Montemayor, scenic Navamuño Reservoir Views, the challenging Climb to Alto del Travieso and Travieso Pass, and the historic The Castañar Monastery. You'll also pass through Montemayor del Río via Horcajo.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling and covers a significant distance on paved and some unpaved roads. While dogs are generally welcome in the natural areas, this specific route is not ideal for bringing a dog due to its length, difficulty, and the nature of road cycling. If you plan to bring a dog, consider shorter, more suitable walking trails in the region.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region experiences varied weather. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for cycling, with milder temperatures and beautiful natural colors. Summers can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winters can bring cold temperatures and potentially snow at higher altitudes, making the climb more challenging or inaccessible.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route, especially since it's in the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de Candelario?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or to access the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de Candelario. However, always respect local regulations, especially regarding protected areas, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in Baños de Montemayor, which serves as the starting and ending point for this circular route. Look for public parking areas within the town, especially near the main roads or town center, where you can safely leave your vehicle.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with or runs alongside several other paths for various segments. You'll find sections that align with trails such as [CIMA CC12] La Garganta (Collado de las Angosturas), [CIMA SA03] El Travieso, Mtb Vía de la Plata, and Vía de la Plata hiking - 02 Merida to Salamanca, among others.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers impressive natural landscapes and magnificent panoramic vistas. You'll get views of the Ambroz Valley and, from higher points, sweeping views of the surrounding mountains and valleys within the Sierra de Béjar and Sierra de Gredos regions. The changing vegetation as you gain altitude also adds to the scenic variety.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or find services in Montemayor del Río?
faqs.answer: Yes, Montemayor del Río is a town along the route where you can expect to find basic services. This may include small shops, cafes, or restaurants where you can refuel and rest before continuing your journey.
